ops.title: getaway journey cash axis is single-component today
ops.body:
# Latent constraint: journey cost `cash` holds at most one component

`_cost_vector` (factors.py) and `_tiebreak` both do `sum(component["amount_cents"] for component in journey["cost"]["cash"])` with NO currency check. This is safe **only because** a journey's `cost.cash` list currently holds at most one element:

- Return legs are structurally always award-mode — a cash leg has no `"detail"` key and `_compose` (journeys.py) accesses `ret["detail"]` unconditionally, so a return can never be a cash leg.
- `_hybrid_specs`/`_expand_hybrid_sides` produce at most one "onward" leg per side.

So `journey["cost"]["cash"]` (built in `_cost`, journeys.py) can hold ≤1 element; summing one component is correct. Per the "no guards against impossible states" doctrine, NO currency-aware guard was added.

**If you ever add a second cash leg** (e.g. an outbound positioning cash hop AND a cash onward hop, possibly in different currencies), revisit `_cost_vector` and `_tiebreak`: summing `amount_cents` across currencies would conflate unlike units into one fungible `$cash` axis and corrupt `_dominates` (primary cost-tier assignment), not just the tie-break. The cost lane would then need a per-currency vector axis (mirroring per-program miles), not a scalar sum.

Surfaced by the rank-finder adversarial 2nd pass (Lead A), 2026-07-14; see [[getaway v2 Phase 9–10: fix wave resolutions + ship]].
